title: Supreme Court Rules on Right to Walk on Footpaths; Congress Seeks Fundamental Right to Vote
summary: The Supreme Court of India passed an order recognising the right of pedestrians to walk safely on footpaths, linking it to the right to life under Article 21. The ruling came against the backdrop of a surge in pedestrian deaths in India, highlighting systemic failures in urban infrastructure. Following this order, the Congress party demanded that the right to vote be declared a fundamental right, seeking to build on the Court's expansive reading of fundamental rights.
exam_angle: Tests understanding of judicial expansion of Article 21 (Right to Life) and the distinction between fundamental rights and constitutional/legal rights. The right to vote is currently a statutory right under the Representation of the People Act, 1951, not a fundamental right.
key_facts: SC linked right to walk on footpaths to Article 21 (Right to Life)
Right to vote is a statutory right under Representation of the People Act, 1951 — not a Fundamental Right
Congress sought elevation of right to vote to a Fundamental Right
India has one of the highest pedestrian fatality rates globally

title: Supreme Court on Police Further Investigation After Final Report Filed
summary: The Supreme Court addressed the legal question of whether police require court permission to conduct further investigation after filing a final report (charge sheet) under the Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC). The Court clarified the procedural requirements under Section 173(8) of the CrPC, which allows further investigation even after a final report is filed. This ruling has implications for criminal procedure and the rights of the accused.
exam_angle: Relevant to GS Paper 2 (Polity/Governance) and for understanding criminal procedure under CrPC; Section 173(8) CrPC is a frequently tested provision regarding further investigation post-charge sheet.
key_facts: Section 173(8) CrPC governs further investigation after final report
SC clarified procedural requirements for police to conduct further investigation
Final report (charge sheet) filing does not necessarily bar further investigation
Ruling impacts rights of accused and procedural fairness

title: India-US Interim Trade Deal Near Finalisation; USTR Greer to Visit New Delhi on June 23–24
summary: India and the United States are in the final stages of an interim bilateral trade deal, with only 'final touches' remaining, according to reports. US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer is scheduled to visit New Delhi on June 23–24, 2025, to conclude negotiations. The deal is expected to address tariff-related issues arising from the Trump administration's reciprocal tariff policy, under which India faces a 26% tariff (currently paused for 90 days).
exam_angle: Tests knowledge of India-US trade relations, the impact of Trump's reciprocal tariff regime on India, and India's trade negotiation strategy. The visit of USTR is a significant diplomatic development.
key_facts: USTR Jamieson Greer to visit New Delhi: June 23–24, 2025
India faces 26% US reciprocal tariff (currently under 90-day pause)
Interim deal described as requiring only 'final touches'
Deal aims to resolve tariff disputes between India and US

title: India's Mega FTA Wave to Bring 60% of Trade Under Preferential Regime
summary: According to an Economic Times analysis, India's ongoing and recently concluded Free Trade Agreements (FTAs) are set to bring approximately 60% of the country's total trade under a preferential tariff regime. This includes FTAs with UAE, Australia, and ongoing negotiations with the EU, UK, and US. The shift marks a major strategic pivot in India's trade policy towards greater bilateral and regional economic integration.
exam_angle: Tests understanding of India's FTA strategy, preferential trade agreements, and their implications for trade policy, domestic industry competitiveness, and WTO obligations.
key_facts: ~60% of India's trade to come under preferential regime via FTAs
Key concluded FTAs: UAE (CEPA), Australia (ECTA/CECA)
Ongoing negotiations: EU, UK, US
Represents major shift in India's trade policy from multilateral to bilateral/regional approach

title: India to Host BRICS Energy Ministers' Meeting in Gurugram on June 25–26
summary: India will host the BRICS Energy Ministers' Meeting in Gurugram (Haryana) on June 25–26, 2025, as part of India's BRICS 2025 Chairmanship. The meeting is expected to focus on energy security, clean energy transitions, and cooperation among BRICS nations on sustainable energy frameworks. India holds the BRICS Chairmanship in 2025 under the theme 'Partnership for Mutually Accelerated Growth, Sustainable Development and Inclusive Multilateralism'.
exam_angle: Tests knowledge of India's BRICS 2025 Chairmanship, BRICS institutional structure, and India's role in global energy governance and multilateral forums.
key_facts: BRICS Energy Ministers' Meeting: June 25–26, 2025, Gurugram
India holds BRICS Chairmanship in 2025
BRICS 2025 theme: 'Partnership for Mutually Accelerated Growth, Sustainable Development and Inclusive Multilateralism'
Focus areas: energy security, clean energy, sustainable development

title: India's International Day of Yoga 2025 Celebrated by AIIA with Diverse Wellness Activities
summary: The All India Institute of Ayurveda (AIIA), under the Ministry of Ayush, celebrated the 12th International Day of Yoga (IDY) 2025 with enthusiastic participation and diverse wellness activities. International Day of Yoga is observed on June 21 every year, a date proposed by Prime Minister Narendra Modi and adopted by the UN General Assembly in December 2014 (Resolution 69/131). IDY 2025 marks the 11th edition (the first was on June 21, 2015).
exam_angle: Tests factual knowledge about International Day of Yoga — its UN recognition, date, theme, and the Ministry of Ayush's role. Note: The headline's mention of '12th IDY 2026' appears erroneous; 2025 is the 11th edition.
key_facts: International Day of Yoga: observed on June 21 every year
UN General Assembly adopted IDY via Resolution 69/131 in December 2014
First IDY celebrated: June 21, 2015
Nodal Ministry: Ministry of Ayush; AIIA is under Ministry of Ayush

title: India's Dependence on Services Exports Increasing: Structural Shift in Trade Composition
summary: A Business Standard analysis highlights a structural shift in India's export composition, with services exports forming an increasingly dominant share relative to merchandise exports. India's services exports — led by IT, business process management, and financial services — have been growing faster than goods exports. This trend raises questions about the diversification of India's export base and vulnerability to external shocks in the services sector.
exam_angle: Relevant to GS Paper 3 (Economy) on India's balance of payments, current account, and export competitiveness. Services account for over 50% of India's GDP and are a key driver of forex earnings.
key_facts: Services now account for a growing share of India's total exports
Key services export segments: IT/ITeS, BPM, financial services, travel
India is among the top 10 global services exporters
Growing services dependence raises concerns about merchandise export diversification

title: Modi-Trump Bilateral at G7 Summit: Trade, Tariffs and Strategic Reset Dominate Agenda
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi met US President Donald Trump on the sidelines of the G7 Summit, with trade, tariffs, and bilateral trust forming the core of discussions. The G7 Summit 2025 is being held in Kananaskis, Canada. The Modi-Trump engagement is significant as India seeks tariff relief from the US's reciprocal tariff regime while deepening strategic ties including defence, technology, and energy cooperation.
exam_angle: Tests knowledge of G7 structure (India is not a G7 member but was invited as an outreach country), India-US bilateral relations, and the trade negotiation context.
key_facts: G7 Summit 2025 held in Kananaskis, Canada
India invited as outreach country (not a G7 member)
Modi-Trump talks focused on trade, tariffs, and strategic partnership
Context: US reciprocal tariff of 26% on India under 90-day pause

title: Iran Conditions Peace Talks with US on Cessation of Lebanon War
summary: Iran stated it will not enter into peace talks with the United States unless the war in Lebanon (involving Israeli operations against Hezbollah) is stopped. This comes amid escalating US-Iran tensions, with President Trump threatening harder action and Iran responding with a warning to 'be careful'. The standoff has implications for Middle East stability, global oil markets, and India's energy security given Iran's role as a potential oil supplier.
exam_angle: Tests awareness of West Asia geopolitics, US-Iran relations, and India's foreign policy considerations in the region. India imports oil from the region and has significant diaspora in West Asia.
key_facts: Iran refuses US peace talks unless Lebanon war is stopped
Trump threatened harder action against Iran
Conflict involves Israeli operations against Hezbollah in Lebanon
West Asia instability impacts India's energy security and diaspora interests

title: West Asia Crisis Pulls Global Startup Funding Down 43% Year-on-Year Since March 2025
summary: Global startup funding has declined by 43% year-on-year since March 2025, with the West Asia crisis cited as a major contributing factor alongside global uncertainty. The contraction has affected Indian startups as well, with investors adopting a risk-off approach. This follows a broader global funding winter that began in 2022 and has seen intermittent recoveries.
exam_angle: Relevant to understanding the Indian startup ecosystem, venture capital trends, and the impact of geopolitical events on capital flows — all relevant for GS Paper 3 (Economy).
key_facts: Global startup funding down 43% YoY since March 2025
West Asia crisis cited as a key trigger for investor risk aversion
Affects Indian startup ecosystem and venture capital inflows
Trend follows the global 'funding winter' that began in 2022

title: Government's AI Strategy Debate: Sovereign AI Models vs. Adoption of Global Platforms
summary: A Business Standard analysis examines the Indian government's Artificial Intelligence strategy, questioning whether investing in sovereign AI models (India-built foundation models) is the optimal approach compared to leveraging existing global platforms. The debate centres on digital sovereignty, data security, and cost-effectiveness. India's IndiaAI Mission (launched in 2024 with Rs 10,372 crore budget) includes support for developing indigenous AI models.
exam_angle: Directly relevant to India's IndiaAI Mission, digital sovereignty, and GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech) questions on AI governance and India's technology policy.
key_facts: IndiaAI Mission approved with budget of Rs 10,372 crore (2024)
Mission includes IndiaAI Compute Capacity, IndiaAI Innovation Centre, and Indigenous AI Model development pillars
Debate: sovereign AI models vs. adoption of existing global AI platforms
Nodal body: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)

title: AI Agents Reshaping India's IT Services Industry Workforce
summary: A Business Standard analysis highlights how AI agents — autonomous software systems capable of performing multi-step tasks — are increasingly being deployed across India's IT services industry, creating a 'silent workforce shift'. Major Indian IT firms including TCS, Infosys, and Wipro are integrating agentic AI into service delivery, potentially affecting employment patterns and service models. This trend has significant implications for India's USD 250+ billion IT exports sector.
exam_angle: Relevant to GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech/Economy) on the impact of emerging technologies on employment, India's IT sector competitiveness, and the future of work.
key_facts: AI agents are autonomous systems capable of multi-step task execution
India's IT services sector (IT exports: ~USD 250 billion in FY2024) is adopting agentic AI
Major adopters: TCS, Infosys, Wipro and other Indian IT majors
Raises concerns about workforce displacement and reskilling needs

title: Nobel Laureate John Jumper Leaves Google DeepMind for Anthropic
summary: John Jumper, who won the 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry (jointly with Demis Hassabis) for developing AlphaFold — an AI system that predicts protein structures — has left Google DeepMind to join Anthropic, the AI safety company backed by Google and Amazon. This represents a significant talent shift in the global AI industry. AlphaFold's protein structure prediction has been called a breakthrough for biology and medicine.
exam_angle: Tests knowledge of the 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry, AlphaFold's significance for science, and awareness of key global AI organisations (DeepMind, Anthropic) — relevant for Persons in News and Sci-Tech.
key_facts: John Jumper: 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry winner (with Demis Hassabis) for AlphaFold
AlphaFold: AI system that predicts 3D protein structures from amino acid sequences
Jumper moves from Google DeepMind to Anthropic
Anthropic is an AI safety company; backed by Google and Amazon
